\caption{\textbf{RandomAgent null distribution, $N=100$ seeds.} Each cell reports the distribution of total return (cumulative return, CR) of an uninformed RandomAgent that draws BUY/SELL/HOLD i.i.d.\ uniformly each trading day, evaluated with the same frictionless, long-only, all-in/all-cash execution as the paper's baselines, over seeds $0$--$99$. Mean$\pm$SD, the 5th/95th percentiles, and the canonical seed=42 draw are shown per (regime, ticker) cell; \emph{seed42 pctile} is the percentile rank of the seed=42 return within that cell's 100-seed distribution. IBOV denotes the Ibovespa index (\texttt{\^{}BVSP}). Across the 20 cells, the seed=42 draw sits at a median percentile of 37.0 (mean 36.3); it falls in a distribution tail ($<$5th or $>$95th pctile) in 4 of 20 cells. This quantifies the dispersion of an uninformed policy, not skill: the wide spreads---largest in the high-volatility \texttt{crisis\_2020} window---reflect the path dependence of random entry/exit timing. A single fixed seed is therefore a noisy point estimate of random-agent performance, which is why downstream portfolio comparisons average over all 100 seeds.}
\label{tab:random-n100}
\end{table}
